summary: Large item-system matching sweep that replaced many small item placeholder functions with C implementations across 97 files. The decomp.dev report recorded 179 new matches, +8572 matched bytes, and matched-code progress from 44.18% to 44.40% (+0.22%). Most work was in src/melee/it/items/*.c plus item headers, with a small Kirby prototype correction. The PR had no body, so motivation beyond the title and bot report is inferred from the diff: harvest many <=52-byte item callbacks by applying repeated item animation, physics, collision, and event-handler patterns.
what_changed: Matched many previously stubbed small item functions, especially UnkMotion*_Anim, UnkMotion*_Phys, UnkMotion*_Coll, and Logic*_event callbacks.
Converted numerous item helper prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures such as void fn_8027DAC8(Item_GObj* gobj), void it_8027DE18(Item_GObj*), and void fn_80286480(Item_GObj*).

Added item-specific variable and attribute structs needed by the matched code, including itSamusBomb_ItemVars, itSeakNeedleThrown_ItemVars, itBox_ItemVars, itFlipper_ItemVars, itLikelikeAttributes, itHammerheadAttributes, itRshellAttributes, and itTaru_ItemVars.
Used GET_ITEM(gobj), GET_JOBJ(gobj), ItemAttr, and itemVar fields to express short state-machine logic, timer decrements, and physics/collision callbacks.
Added includes such as it/inlines.h and it/it_266F.h in item source files to access item accessors and shared item collision/physics helpers.
Updated one fighter header prototype: ftKb_SpecialN_800F5800 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftKb_SpecialN_800F5800(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*).
smart_moves: Targeted a bounded class of low-risk functions by byte size, allowing a broad progress gain without needing to solve large control-flow-heavy routines.

Reused repeated item callback idioms: simple Anim functions return false or expire on timer, Phys functions call it_80272860 with x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max, and Coll functions call shared helpers then return false.
Unlocked many matches by replacing vague UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete Item_GObj* signatures, making callback call sites compile naturally and avoiding argument-mismatch placeholders.
Added only the struct fields needed by observed accesses rather than over-modeling whole item data layouts, preserving matching while improving type information.
Kept empty matched functions explicit where appropriate, such as no-op Phys callbacks, instead of leaving placeholder comments.
decomp_lessons: Small item functions often fall into highly reusable patterns; once the helper signatures are known, many files can be matched by applying the same animation, physics, and collision templates.

For item Phys callbacks, the common falling-physics pattern is Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj), ItemAttr* attrs = ip->xCC_item_attr, then it_80272860(gobj, attrs->x10_fall_speed, attrs->x14_fall_speed_max).

For item Coll callbacks, helpers such as it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, it_8026E32C, it_8026E7E0, and it_8026E8C4 frequently take the current Item_GObj* plus transition/callback functions and then the wrapper returns false.
Timer-based small Anim callbacks commonly test a life timer or itemVar counter, return true when expired, otherwise decrement and return false.
Concrete prototypes in headers are not just cleanup; they enable correct callback passing and prevent missed arguments in short functions.

When a matched function calls another item helper, verify whether the helper expects gobj even if the decompiled body initially appears callable without arguments; review caught two missing gobj arguments.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks follow names like itBombhei_UnkMotion5_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotion10_Coll, itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Anim, and itTaru_UnkMotion5_Phys.
Item logic event callbacks follow names like it_3F14_Logic6_Clanked, it_2725_Logic13_DmgDealt, and it_3F14_Logic42_PickedUp.
Address-based helper names remain in the it_802xxxxx or fn_802xxxxx style until semantic names are known.
Item variable structs use item-name prefixes and suffix _ItemVars, with field names preserving itemVar offsets such as xDD4, xDD8, xDDC, xDE0.
Attribute structs use item-name prefixes and suffix Attributes or DatAttrs, preserving offset-like field names where semantics are unknown.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Batch-match short functions by recognizing common assembly shapes: load Item from gobj, load ItemAttr, call shared helper, return false/true.
Use exact return types for callbacks: many Coll and Anim wrappers are bool and return false after a helper call; many transition helpers are void(Item_GObj*).
For struct-dependent matches, add minimal fields at the exact itemVar offsets accessed by the assembly rather than guessing full semantics.
For collision callbacks, match argument order carefully; examples in the diff include it_8026D62C(gobj, callback), it_8026E15C(gobj, callback), and it_8026E8C4(gobj, callback_a, callback_b).
Review-generated corrections show that omitting gobj in helper calls can still produce near-looking code but is wrong for the intended function signature.

review_feedback.feedback: Suggested passing gobj to it_802874F0 in itTaru_UnkMotion5_Phys: it_802874F0(gobj);
Suggested passing gobj to it_802EC1F4 in it_2725_Logic13_DmgDealt: it_802EC1F4(gobj);

follow_up_queries: Find remaining item functions just above 52 bytes that use the same Anim/Phys/Coll helper patterns.
Audit item helper prototypes still using UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS where call sites pass Item_GObj* callbacks.
Search for Coll wrappers that call it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, it_8026E32C, it_8026E7E0, or it_8026E8C4 and return false.
Search for lifeTimer or xDD8 countdown Anim callbacks that can be matched with the timer-decrement pattern.
Review itemVar structs in itCommonItems.h and itCharItems.h for newly inferred fields that can support larger follow-up matches.
